title: Inside the diplomacy reshaping regional trade
dek: Negotiators in Addis Ababa are tackling the unglamorous details that determine whether free trade works.
author: Selam Bekele
body: Trade agreements are signed in conference halls, but they succeed or fail at border posts. Negotiators are now focusing on shared documents, product standards and faster systems for resolving disputes.
A shipment delayed by incompatible paperwork can erase the advantage of a lower tariff.
Progress will be measured by whether a small producer can predict the time and cost of reaching a customer across the border.
